title: Property Management Operations Score
subtitle: Find the exact workflows where your team is losing hours every week

persona_selector.text: How many units does your company currently manage?

answers.label: Under 250 units
250 to 750 units
750 to 2,000 units
Over 2,000 units

questions.text: How are inbound tenant requests (maintenance, questions, complaints) captured today?

answers.label: Phone calls and personal inboxes, no central log
Shared inbox but no tagging or routing rules
Portal plus email, manually triaged daily
Portal with automated ticket creation and assignment
Unified intake across portal, SMS, email with AI triage and routing

questions.text: How long does it take on average to send a first response to a tenant request?

answers.label: More than 24 hours, often slips through
Within 24 hours if someone catches it
Within 8 business hours most days
Within 1 hour during business hours
Instant automated acknowledgment, human follow-up within 1 hour

questions.text: How much weekly time does your office team spend answering repetitive tenant questions (rent due dates, policies, portal logins)?

answers.label: 15+ hours per week
8 to 15 hours per week
4 to 8 hours per week
1 to 4 hours per week
Under 1 hour, handled by self-serve or AI assistant

questions.text: Are tenant conversations logged against the unit or lease record automatically?

answers.label: No logging, lives in individual inboxes
Manually copied in occasionally
Logged when the team remembers, inconsistent
Mostly automated with some manual cleanup
Every channel auto-syncs to the tenant record

questions.text: How do you handle after-hours and weekend tenant inquiries?

answers.label: They wait until Monday morning
On-call staff checks phone sporadically
Answering service routes true emergencies only
24/7 coverage with clear escalation rules
AI assistant handles routine questions, escalates emergencies instantly

low.headline: Your team is drowning in tenant messages
low.text: You are losing 15 to 25 hours a week to manual triage and repetitive answers. This is the single highest-leverage area to fix first because volume is relentless and costs compound daily.
steps: Consolidate every inbound channel (phone, email, portal, SMS) into one shared inbox with tagging
Build an FAQ knowledge base covering the top 20 repeat questions and deploy it at the portal level
Pilot an AI assistant on one channel to auto-answer routine questions and log every thread to the tenant record

mid.headline: Solid intake, but response speed and logging still leak hours
mid.text: You have the foundation but the cracks are in triage speed and conversation history. Closing those gaps turns a good system into one that scales past 1,000 units without adding headcount.
steps: Add automated acknowledgment and SLA timers to every ticket type
Route requests by category and urgency using rules, not human judgment
Sync all channels to the unit record so any staffer can see full history in one click

high.headline: Communication is a strength, now push it to a moat
high.text: You are in the top tier here. The next gain is turning response data into predictive insight so you can prevent issues before tenants even write in.
steps: Layer sentiment analysis on incoming messages to flag churn risks early
Use request volume by property to score asset health and staff load weekly
Productize your playbook internally so new hires hit full speed in under 30 days

questions.text: How are new leases generated and sent for signature?

answers.label: Word templates, manually edited each time
Templates with manual field entry, emailed for wet signature
PDF templates with e-signature tool, manual data entry
Auto-populated from CRM and sent via e-signature
End-to-end automated from application approval to countersigned lease

questions.text: How do you track lease renewals and expirations?

answers.label: Spreadsheet or memory, often caught late
Calendar reminders set manually
Property management software alerts, worked ad hoc
Automated renewal workflow starting 90 days out
Full renewal automation with pricing, outreach, and e-sign handled by system

questions.text: How are executed leases and tenant documents stored and retrieved?

answers.label: Physical files or scattered cloud folders
Shared drive with inconsistent naming
Centralized in PM software but requires manual upload
Auto-filed to the unit record after signing
Fully indexed, searchable by clause, date, or tenant instantly

questions.text: How is compliance (insurance certificates, addendums, local disclosures) tracked?

answers.label: Not tracked, relies on memory
Checked manually at move-in only
Tracked in a spreadsheet with quarterly review
Automated reminders when documents expire
Automated collection, verification, and renewal across the portfolio

low.headline: Lease ops are bleeding rent days and compliance risk
low.text: Every day a lease sits unsigned is lost rent, and every missing document is a liability. Your current process is burning 8 to 12 hours a week and exposing the business to avoidable penalties.
steps: Move every lease template into an e-signature platform with auto-filled merge fields
Build a 90/60/30 day renewal workflow that triggers outreach automatically
Centralize all executed documents in one system with consistent naming and tenant tagging

mid.headline: Core lease flow works, but renewals and compliance still need hands
mid.text: You have the basics automated, but the high-value edges (renewal timing, compliance tracking) are still manual. Tightening these recovers 5 to 8 hours weekly and protects the rent roll.
steps: Automate renewal pricing recommendations using market data plus tenant payment history
Set up automated insurance and compliance document collection with expiry reminders
Connect lease signing to onboarding so welcome emails, portal access, and utility setup fire automatically

high.headline: Document ops are tight, squeeze the last mile
high.text: You are running lease management like an enterprise. Next gain is using document data as a decision-making asset, not just a filing requirement.
steps: Extract lease clause data into a searchable database for portfolio-level reporting
Auto-flag non-standard clauses or risky terms during signing
Benchmark renewal rates by property manager and use it to refine playbooks quarterly

questions.text: How are maintenance requests routed to the right vendor or technician?

answers.label: Office manager reads the request and calls someone
Manual assignment based on vendor list
Categorized in software, manually dispatched
Auto-assigned by trade and property with approval step
Fully automated dispatch with vendor scoring and SLA tracking

questions.text: How do you confirm work orders are actually completed?

answers.label: We ask the vendor and trust the answer
Tenant complaints tell us if something is still broken
Photo upload required on close-out
Photos plus tenant confirmation required before invoice paid
Multi-point verification with automated tenant satisfaction check

questions.text: How is vendor performance measured across your portfolio?

answers.label: Gut feel, we use who we like
Anecdotal from staff feedback
Basic spreadsheet of jobs per vendor
Dashboard with response time, cost, and completion rate
Real-time vendor scorecards driving auto-assignment

questions.text: How do invoices from vendors get reviewed and approved?

answers.label: Paper or email, reviewed when someone has time
Manually matched against work orders
Digital invoices uploaded, manually approved
Auto-matched to work order with approval workflow
End-to-end automated from receipt to payment with variance flagging

low.headline: Maintenance is your biggest hidden cost
low.text: Manual routing, weak close-out, and gut-feel vendor choice are costing you on both time and repair bills. This is where PM companies most often find 15+ hours a week and thousands in overbilling.
steps: Force every request through one intake so nothing comes in by text or phone unlogged
Require photo plus tenant confirmation before any invoice is approved
Build a vendor scorecard tracking response time, first-time fix rate, and cost per job type

mid.headline: You have process, now add intelligence
mid.text: Your system works but still leans on human judgment at the wrong moments. Moving dispatch and invoice review to rule-based automation saves 6 to 10 hours weekly and improves repair quality.
steps: Auto-assign work orders by trade, urgency, and vendor performance score
Set invoice variance rules that auto-approve routine jobs and flag outliers for review
Send automated post-job satisfaction surveys and feed results back into vendor ranking

high.headline: Maintenance is running clean, go predictive
high.text: You are ahead of almost every PM operator your size. Next move is using historical work order data to predict and prevent issues across the portfolio.
steps: Model recurring issues by unit age, asset type, and vendor to forecast capital needs
Trigger preventative maintenance automatically based on equipment service history
Give owners a real-time maintenance performance dashboard as a retention and upsell tool

questions.text: How is rent collected and reconciled each month?

answers.label: Mix of checks, transfers, and cash, manually posted
Online portal plus manual check entry
Portal is primary, reconciled weekly
Automated posting and daily reconciliation
Fully automated with real-time dashboards and exception flagging

questions.text: How do you handle late payments and collections?

answers.label: Staff manually calls and emails late tenants
Template reminders sent manually
Automated first reminder, manual follow-up
Full automated cadence through legal handoff
Automated cadence with AI-personalized outreach based on payment history

questions.text: How are owner statements and distributions produced?

answers.label: Manually built in spreadsheets each month
Generated from software then edited by hand
Software-generated, reviewed before sending
Fully automated with owner portal access
Real-time owner dashboards plus automated monthly statements

questions.text: How long does your month-end close take?

answers.label: More than 10 business days
6 to 10 business days
3 to 5 business days
Under 3 business days
Essentially real-time, close is a formality

low.headline: Your accounting stack is slowing the whole business down
low.text: Late close, manual collections, and handmade statements eat 20+ hours a month and make owner relationships harder than they need to be. This is foundational and must be fixed early.
steps: Move all rent to the portal with auto-posting and kill check acceptance wherever possible
Build an automated late payment cadence covering day 1, day 5, day 10, and legal handoff
Switch owner statements to auto-generated reports delivered through a self-serve portal

mid.headline: Close cycle and collections still have manual drag
mid.text: The bones are good but you are still touching too many transactions by hand. Automating reconciliation and owner reporting shortens close and frees your accounting lead for strategic work.
steps: Set up daily auto-reconciliation with rules that flag only true exceptions
Personalize collections messages using tenant payment history and communication preference
Give owners real-time access to performance data to cut inbound questions by more than half

high.headline: Accounting is dialed in, now make it a growth asset
high.text: You are running a finance operation most PMs envy. Use that strength to win and retain owners by turning data into insight.
steps: Build owner-facing benchmarks comparing their property to the portfolio
Forecast delinquency risk per tenant using payment and communication signals
Package your close process into a service tier or differentiator in sales conversations

questions.text: How are prospect inquiries captured and followed up on?

answers.label: Calls and emails, often missed or delayed
Inquiries logged manually in a CRM
Inquiries auto-captured, manually followed up
Automated follow-up sequence with lead scoring
AI-driven outreach, scheduling, and nurture across every channel

questions.text: How is property showing scheduling handled?

answers.label: Phone tag with prospects
Back-and-forth email scheduling
Self-serve scheduling link sent manually
Automated booking integrated with calendars
Self-tour technology with automated qualification and follow-up

questions.text: How are rental applications processed?

answers.label: Paper or PDF, reviewed manually
Online form with manual screening
Online application with third-party screening report
Auto-screened with rules-based approve, decline, or review
End-to-end automated approval with standardized criteria and audit trail

questions.text: How is the move-in experience managed?

answers.label: Staff walks through everything manually
Checklist emailed, manual follow-up
Onboarding emails triggered at key dates
Full digital move-in (keys, portal, utilities) coordinated automatically
Guided onboarding app with real-time support and instant issue resolution

low.headline: You are losing leads and slowing move-ins
low.text: Every prospect who waits more than an hour is half as likely to convert, and a rough onboarding sets the tone for the whole tenancy. Manual leasing is probably costing you days of vacancy on every unit.
steps: Deploy auto-responders and lead capture across every listing source within the first 5 minutes
Switch to self-serve scheduling integrated with your leasing calendar
Build a 14-day automated move-in journey covering keys, portal, utilities, and first-week check-in

mid.headline: Leasing is organized, speed and conversion still have room
mid.text: You are capturing leads and onboarding cleanly, but conversion speed and prospect qualification still lean on people. Tightening these often cuts vacancy days by 3 to 7 per unit.
steps: Add lead scoring so your leasing team focuses only on high-intent prospects
Automate screening decisions for clear approvals and clear declines, route only the gray zone to humans
Layer a digital move-in experience that collects photos, signatures, and setup confirmations automatically

high.headline: Leasing is a competitive advantage, lock in the lead
high.text: You are converting faster than your competitors. Next move is turning leasing data into a forecasting tool and training loop.
steps: Predict leasing velocity by property and price point, feed it into pricing decisions weekly
Use onboarding NPS data to refine staff performance and process quarterly
Productize your leasing engine as a selling point in owner acquisition pitches

cta.headline: Ready to turn your score into 20+ hours back every week?
cta.description: Book a 30-minute strategy call and I will map your top 3 automation opportunities based on your score, show you what the fix looks like, and give you a clear order of operations. No pitch, just a plan you can run with or hand to your team.
